channel.name: Quorvex AI
channel.positioning: Self-hosted AI testing agents that turn specs, PRDs, and app exploration into validated Playwright tests your team can inspect, commit, and run in CI.

defaults.audience: Playwright teams, QA automation engineers, self-hosted devtool users, and engineering teams evaluating AI testing tools
defaults.voice_style: direct senior-engineer explanation with low hype and concrete product proof
defaults.avatar_policy: Use AI avatar only for hook, transitions, and outro. Main footage must be real Quorvex usage.

episodes.title: Quorvex AI in 10 Minutes: Generate Your First Playwright Test
episodes.promise: Show the fastest credible path from repo setup to a generated Playwright test.
episodes.audience_problem: Teams want AI to speed up test creation, but they still need readable Playwright code they can own.
episodes.cta: Star Quorvex AI on GitHub and try the minimal setup path.
screen_flow: Open GitHub repo and docs landing page.
Show minimal or Docker setup command.
Create or show a plain-English test spec.

episodes.title: Why AI-Generated Tests Fail, and How Quorvex Validates Them
episodes.promise: Explain why prompt-only generated tests are fragile and how browser validation changes the workflow.
episodes.audience_problem: AI can draft tests, but drafts are not useful if selectors, waits, and assumptions are never executed.
episodes.cta: Compare Quorvex against your current Playwright workflow.

episodes.title: From Plain-English Spec to Playwright Code
episodes.promise: Teach the spec format and show how a clear test request becomes reviewable code.
episodes.audience_problem: Teams need a simple authoring format that QA and engineers can both review.
episodes.cta: Write one spec from a real workflow and run it locally.

episodes.title: Quorvex Dashboard Walkthrough
episodes.promise: Map the dashboard to the real QA workflow: specs, runs, requirements, coverage, agents, and analytics.
episodes.audience_problem: A testing platform is only useful if teams can see status, evidence, ownership, and next actions.
episodes.cta: Open the dashboard locally and follow the same route.

episodes.title: Self-Hosted Setup with Docker
episodes.promise: Show a practical local self-hosted setup and explain which services matter.
episodes.audience_problem: Teams want AI testing without sending their workflow into a closed hosted platform.
episodes.cta: Run the minimal setup first, then move to the full stack when needed.

episodes.title: PRD to Tests: Turning Product Requirements into Coverage
episodes.promise: Show how product requirements become candidate tests and coverage signals.
episodes.audience_problem: Product docs often drift away from actual test coverage.
episodes.cta: Try the flow with one small PRD before using it for a release.

episodes.title: API Testing with OpenAPI Import
episodes.promise: Show how Quorvex can turn API descriptions into test coverage.
episodes.audience_problem: Teams need API checks next to UI workflows, not in a disconnected tool.
episodes.cta: Import one OpenAPI file and inspect the generated test before running it.

episodes.title: CI/CD Quality Gates with Generated Playwright Tests
episodes.promise: Show how generated tests become normal CI assets and how quality gates fit into pull requests.
episodes.audience_problem: AI test generation is not enough unless the output can be reviewed and run in existing CI.
episodes.cta: Commit one generated test and wire it into your normal Playwright CI job.
